title: Gasteranthus perennis
intro: Gasteranthus perennis is a flowering plant species in the Borge family (Boraginaceae), native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory problems, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, which contribute to its potential health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Gasteranthus perennis include alkaloids, terpenoids, and phenolic compounds, such as glycosides and flavonoids, which have been reported to exhibit antimicrobial, antioxidant, and anti-inflammatory activities. The most common herbal preparations made with Gasteranthus perennis include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, from fever and cough to skin and respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Gasteranthus perennis is a genus of flowering plants in the family Bignonaceae. The species Gasteranthus perennis is a perennial epiphyte native to Ecuador. It belongs to the kingdom Plantae, and more specifically to the class Magnoliopsida. The order is Asterales, and the family is indeed Bignonaceae. Gasteranthus perennis is further classified into the subfamily Colletioideae, and the genus Gasteranthus.
